hooks.command: bash -c 'if [ -d ".quality/checkpoints" ]; then bash "${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/scripts/checkpoint.sh" drift 2>&1 || { echo "BLOCKED: Quality checkpoints have drifted — run gates before committing"; exit 1; }; fi'

hooks.prompt:
<output-format>Respond with ONLY valid JSON matching this schema:
{"type": "object", "required": ["ok"], "properties": {"ok": {"type": "boolean"}, "reason": {"type": "string", "description": "Required when ok is false. One sentence."}}, "additionalProperties": false}
No markdown. No explanation. No code fences. Just the JSON object.</output-format>

<task>Review code in $ARGUMENTS for quality violations.</task>

<block-rules>
- jest.mock()/vi.mock() targeting the module under test
- spyOn().mockImplementation()/.mockReturnValue()/.mockResolvedValue() on internal functions
- @patch()/unittest.mock.patch() on the function being tested
- jest.fn().mockReturnValue() replacing internal functions
- # noqa, # type: ignore, // eslint-disable, // eslint-disable-next-line, @ts-ignore, @ts-expect-error, or any lint suppression
</block-rules>

<allow-rules>
- Mocking external boundaries (HTTP clients, third-party APIs, timers, Date.now())
- Real spies with NO .mock*() chain
- Documentation or comments explaining anti-patterns
- The mock target is clearly an external dependency (fetch, axios, database client, SDK)
</allow-rules>
